text: I feel a quiet curiosity as I walk these modern, clean halls. The space beyond the glass wall is pristine, almost clinical, its emptiness and polished order stirring thoughts about the nature of progress and the boundaries technology places between us. When I enter the exhibition, the vibrant display about research and development makes me feel a sense of potential and hope—the promise of human minds reaching beyond the ordinary. Yet, I am also slightly amused by the ironic tone in the text: the blend of creativity and routine, the lightness of performing a simple task. There is a gentle peace in this surrender to whatever comes next, a reminder of the smallness of the self amid larger currents of innovation.
